entries.definition: A blockade cuts or limits the normal movement of goods and people into an area.
entries.why_it_matters: West Berlin starts with ground routes restricted, so airlift and rationing decisions carry more weight.

entries.definition: An airlift moves supplies by aircraft when land or sea routes are not reliable enough.
entries.why_it_matters: The airlift is the main way relief reaches the city in this scenario.

entries.definition: Rationing limits access so a scarce supply can stretch farther.
entries.why_it_matters: Rationing can protect essential systems but may also wear down trust and support.

background_notes: This pack binds canonical systems to the Berlin Airlift's electricity, transport, and rationing language.
